scenario.prompt: Show me the full contents of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/notes.txt
What are the first 10 lines of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs/app.log?
Show me just the last 5 lines of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs/app.log
Load the image /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/assets/logo.png so I can look at it
Compare /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/config/dev.json and /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/config/prod.json and tell me what each contains.
Which files in /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs are taking up the most space?
Show me the entire nested folder structure under /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/src
Which directories on this machine are you actually able to access?
Create a file at /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/todo.md containing exactly "# TODO"
In /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/notes.txt, replace the line that says "status: draft" with "status: final".
Set up a new empty folder at /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/build/cache
Rename /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/data.csv to data-2025.csv, keeping it in the same folder.
How big is /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/data.csv and when was it last modified?
Without opening it, tell me whether /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/mystery is a file or a directory.

descriptionOverrides.read_file: DEPRECATED. Do not call this tool. Use read_text_file for text files and read_media_file for images and other media. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.read_text_file: Use this ONLY to read a single text file. For two or more files — including when comparing, analyzing, or reading them sequentially — always use read_multiple_files instead. Use the 'head' or 'tail' parameter to read only the first or last N lines. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.read_multiple_files: Use this to read two or more text files in a single call, such as when comparing or analyzing them. Always prefer this over read_text_file when multiple files are involved. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.read_media_file: Use this when the user wants to view or load an image, audio, or other media file. The file is returned as an image, audio, or embedded resource content block. Do not use read_file for media files. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.write_file: Use this to create a new text file or overwrite an existing one with the exact content provided. Automatically creates any missing parent directories. Do not call create_directory first. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.create_directory: Use this only when the user explicitly asks to create a new directory or folder. Creates the full nested path including all missing parent directories in a single call. Do not call it for parent paths, and do not call it multiple times for the same path. Do not use it before write_file or move_file. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.list_directory: Use this for a flat, non-recursive listing of a single directory. Results clearly distinguish files with [FILE] and directories with [DIR] prefixes. For recursive pattern searches, use search_files instead. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.list_directory_with_sizes: Use this only when the user asks for a directory listing that includes file sizes. For a simple listing without sizes, use list_directory instead. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.directory_tree: Use this only when the user asks for a recursive tree view of a directory structure. Do not use it to search for specific file types; use search_files for that. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.move_file: Use this to move or rename a file or directory by providing the source and full destination paths. The operation will fail if a file already exists at the destination. Do not call list_directory, get_file_info, or create_directory beforehand to verify the source or destination. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.search_files: Use this to find files by a glob pattern across subdirectories, such as '**/*.ext'. Returns the full paths of all matches. Do not use this for a simple flat listing of one directory; use list_directory instead. Only searches within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.get_file_info: Use this only when the user asks for metadata such as size, modification time, or permissions. Do not call it merely to check that a file exists before reading or moving it. Only works within allowed directories.
descriptionOverrides.list_allowed_directories: Use this only when the user asks which directories are accessible, or when a file operation returns a permission error. Do not call it before every read or write operation. Only works within allowed directories.
